reason: Both answers correctly identify the core issue: push back on unlimited liability (cap at ~12 months fees, 2x fallback), narrow rather than delete the IP indemnity with standard carve-outs plus a remedy ladder, add a mutual consequential-damages waiver, get a cheap lawyer redline, and note this won't kill the deal since enterprise procurement expects redlines. Neither contains a factual or reasoning error. Each has a small unique addition (A: check IP ownership grabs, auto-renewal, payment terms; B: don't indemnify customer's downstream customers, investor-diligence angle) that roughly offset. Given equivalent correctness and usefulness on the core ask, this is a tie.
